name: Cppcheck Open-Source C/C++ Static Analyzer
what_it_does: Open-source static analysis tool for C and C++ code — enabling developers and security engineers to detect undefined behavior, memory leaks, null pointer dereferences, buffer overflows, and other C/C++ bugs without compilation, running as a standalone CLI tool with XML/JSON output, CI/CD integration, and IDE plugins, with a unique focus on detecting actual bugs with low false-positive rate rather than style issues.
use_cases: Detecting C/C++ memory bugs via Cppcheck CLI in CI/CD pipelines for embedded development
Finding undefined behavior in C++ code via Cppcheck for cross-platform compatibility
Integrating Cppcheck XML reports into SonarQube or Jenkins for dashboard visualization
Running Cppcheck as pre-commit check for C/C++ projects to catch bugs early
Supplementing compiler warnings with additional bug detection via Cppcheck
not_for: Enterprise SAST with policy management (use Klocwork or Coverity for enterprise C++ SAST)
Security vulnerability scanning with CVE database (Cppcheck is bug detection, not CVE mapping)
Languages other than C/C++ (use Semgrep or language-specific tools for other languages)
best_when: A C/C++ developer team needs a free, zero-configuration static analyzer to detect common bugs — Cppcheck provides immediate value with minimal setup and low false-positive rate.
avoid_when: You need enterprise policy management, CVE mapping, MISRA compliance, or multi-language SAST.

auth.notes: No authentication required. CLI tool — no API service. Run locally: cppcheck --enable=all source.cpp. No network calls for analysis. Output: text (stdout), XML (--xml), JSON (via addon). IDE integration: Eclipse, VS Code, CLion via plugins. CMake integration available. No REST API — purely local tool.

pricing.notes: Created by Daniel Marjamäki (Swedish). GPL v3 license. GitHub: danmar/cppcheck (6,000+ stars). Source: SourceForge + GitHub. Focus on zero false positives for listed checkers (unlike most tools that optimize for coverage). Checks: memory leaks, null pointer, uninitialized variables, array out of bounds, invalid formatting strings, dead code, thread safety. Suppressions via inline comments (// cppcheck-suppress). Platform options: unix32, unix64, win32, win64. CMakeLists.txt integration. Misra addon (requires Python). Premium version: Cppcheck Premium (commercial support by Daniel Marjamäki).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool only — no API service. GPL v3. No auth. XML/JSON output. C/C++ only. Low false-positive philosophy. MISRA addon available. Local execution only.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Analysis is deterministic — same code always produces same results. Fully idempotent. No state stored.
known_agent_gotchas: No REST API — this is a local CLI tool, not a network service
Low false-positive philosophy: not all possible bugs reported — supplement with compiler warnings (-Wall -Wextra)
MISRA addon requires Python: python3 misra.py --rule-texts misra.txt file.dump
Suppressions via inline comment: // cppcheck-suppress nullPointer
For CI integration: --error-exitcode=1 exits with non-zero code on findings

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials required — zero auth attack surface. GPL v3 open source — fully auditable. Offline operation — no code leaves developer machine. Low false-positive approach avoids alert fatigue. Purely local execution. C/C++ memory safety analysis catches critical vulnerability classes (buffer overflow, use-after-free).
